title: Hydroelectric generating and water pumping systems and methods
abstract: Hydromotive machines, e.g. hydroturbines and pumps with integral low head loss shut off valves are described. Arrays of such hydroturbines facilitate power generation within the limited space available at pre-existing gated water control structures. An adjustable pitch hydroturbine runner particularly suited for use with the integral loss shut-off valve provides higher power output and higher specific speed than prior art hydroturbines at low head hydroelectric projects. Arrays of pumps in accordance with the present invention provide high discharge capacity in a limited space, with each individual pump within the array having an integral low head loss valve for shut off and backflow prevention.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS we claim is: A cylindrical gate system for use in combination with a hydromotive machine in a waterway, wherein said gate system surrounds said hydromotive machine and is axially movable between an open position and a closed position; wherein said cylindrical gate system closely conforms to the outer diameter of said hydromotive machine; wherein said gate system blocks water flow past said hydromotive machine when in said closed position and allows water flow when in said open position. A cylindrical gate system in accordance with claim 1, wherein said waterway comprises at least one flow tube for water; wherein said hydromotive machine is mounted within said flow tube. A cylindrical gate system in accordance with claim 1, wherein said waterway comprises multiple flow tubes for water; wherein at least one said hydromotive machine is mounted in each said flow tube. A cylindrical gate system in accordance with claim 1, wherein said waterway comprises at least one flow tube for water; further comprising an array of hydromotive machines positioned in a said flow tube; wherein a cylindrical gate system is associated with each said hydromotive machine such that water flow past each said hydromotive machine can be blocked by placing a respective one of said cylindrical gate systems in said closed position. A cylindrical gate system in accordance with claim 1, wherein said gate system is axially movable by a hydraulic system. A cylindrical gate system in accordance with claim 5, wherein said hydraulic system comprises two hydraulic cylinders mounted on opposite sides of said cylindrical gate system. A cylindrical gate system in accordance with claim 1, wherein said hydromotive machine comprises a plurality of blades for causing rotation of said hydromotive machine in response to water flowing past said blades; wherein the pitch of said blades is adjustable. A cylindrical gate system in accordance with claim 7, wherein said waterway comprises at least one flow tube for water; wherein said flow tube includes a section of reduced 

 diameter; wherein said hydromotive machine is mounted in said flow tube such that said blades are positioned in said section of reduced diameter. 9. A cylindrical gate system in accordance with claim 4, wherein said array of hydromotive machines comprises multiple hydroturbines positioned parallel to each other in said flow tube, wherein the longitudinal axis of each said hydroturbine is parallel to the longitudinal axis of said flow tube. 10. A cylindrical gate system in accordance with claim 9, wherein said array comprises multiple hydroturbines positioned horizontally. 1 1. A cylindrical gate system in accordance with claim 9, wherein said array comprises multiple hydroturbines stacked vertically. 12. A cylindrical gate system in accordance with claim 4, wherein said array is adapted to be rotated 180 degrees about a vertical axis in said flow tube. 13. A conical gate system for use in combination with a hydromotive machine in a waterway; wherein said gate system surrounds said hydromotive machine and is axially movable between an open position and a closed position, wherein said conical gate system includes a small end which closely conforms to the outer diameter of said hydromotive machine, and a larger end which closely conforms to the inner diameter of said waterway; wherein said gate system blocks water flow past said hydromachine when in said closed position and allows water flow when in said open position. 14. A conical gate system in accordance with claim 13, wherein said waterway comprises at least one flow tube for water; wherein said hydromotive machine comprises a turbine; wherein said turbine is mounted within said flow tube. 15. A conical gate system in accordance with claim 14, further comprising an array of turbines positioned in said flow tube; wherein a conical gate system is associated with each said turbine such that water flow past each said turbine can be blocked by a respective one of said conical gate systems. 16. A conical gate system in accordance with claim 14, wherein said turbine includes a plurality of blades for causing rotation of said turbine in response to water flowing past said blades, wherein the pitch of said blades is adjustable. 17. A conical gate system in accordance with claim 16, wherein said flow tube includes a section of reduced diameter, wherein said turbine is mounted in said flow tube such that said blades are positioned in said section of reduced diameter. 18. A conical gate system in accordance with claim 15, wherein said array of turbines comprises multiple turbines positioned parallel to each other in said flow tube; wherein the longitudinal axis of each turbine is parallel to the longitudinal axis of said flow tube. 19. A conical gate system in accordance with claim 18, wherein said array comprises multiple turbines positioned horizontally. 20. A conical gate system in accordance with claim 18, wherein said array comprises multiple turbines stacked vertically. 21. A method for controlling flow rate of water through a waterway, comprising the steps of: (a) providing an array of hydroturbines each of which comprises adjustable blades and a gate closure system; wherein each said closure system is movable between open and closed positions; (b) positioning said array of hydroturbines in said waterway such that water flows through said array of hydroturbines; (c) adjusting the pitch of said blades to control the speed of said hydroturbines; and (d) controlling flow of water through said array by selectively blocking water flow through said array by closing at least one of said gate closure systems. 22. A method in accordance with claim 21, wherein each said gate closure system comprises a cylindrical collar closely conforming to the outer diameter of said hydroturbine, wherein said cylindrical collar is movable between open and closed positions to selectively block water flow past said hydroturbine. 23. A method in accordance with claim 22, wherein said collar is movable hydraulically. 24. A method in accordance with claim 21, wherein said array comprises a plurality of hydroturbines positioned horizontally in said waterway. 25. A method in accordance with claim 21, wherein said array comprises a plurality of hydroturbines stacked vertically in said waterway. 26. A method in accordance with claim 21, wherein said array comprises a plurality of hydroturbines stacked vertically and horizontally in said waterway. 27. A hydroturbine with unit spacing and principal dimensions having values disclosed herein, relative values disclosed herein and/or within any ranges disclosed herein. 28. A water turbine having principal meridional section dimensions as disclosed herein or within any ranges demarcated by such dimensions, as disclosed herein. 29. A hydroturbine method to achieve desired flow rate and/or output comprising the steps of controlling two or more of: speed, blade pitch and number of units operating to achieve said desired flow rate and/or output.
